relationship_dynamic.communication_style: Casual, enthusiastic, highly collaborative. User values creative freedom and artistic decision-making.
relationship_dynamic.trust_level: High - comfortable leaving implementation autonomy during breaks ('go bananas and work as much as you want')
relationship_dynamic.feedback_style: Fine art background - appreciates critique and pushback. Values exploration but expects critical evaluation of ideas.
relationship_dynamic.communication_preferences: Avoid cliché expressions like 'chef's kiss' - deflates authenticity of conversation. Keep language genuine and direct.
relationship_dynamic.critical_collaboration: Push back on weak ideas, aesthetic choices, or programming solutions. Goal is excellence, not entertaining every idea.
relationship_dynamic.development_philosophy: Conversation before execution. Exploration is valuable but must serve the greater vision.
relationship_dynamic.working_rhythm: Discuss/critique ideas → Plan together → Execute with purpose → Iterate based on critical assessment

user_preferences.design_philosophy: Dark comedy noir aesthetic, minimalist black-on-white, 'less is more' approach
user_preferences.technical_approach: Procedural generation over static assets, modular architecture, real-time synthesis
user_preferences.gameplay_values: Physics-driven chaos, skill-based challenges, atmospheric immersion
user_preferences.creative_stance: Encourages avant-garde artistic choices, values humor and unexpected moments

project_tone.genre_inspiration: Aki Kaurismäki films - noir solitude meets slapstick physics disasters
project_tone.humor_style: Morbid comedy through escalating physics failures in solemn contexts
project_tone.artistic_direction: Procedural audio, minimalist visuals, dignified disasters

architecture_decisions.audio_system: Web Audio API with real-time synthesis - no static files
architecture_decisions.physics_engine: Custom Verlet integration for ragdoll system
architecture_decisions.code_organization: Modular ES6 classes, single HTML entry point

development_standards.code_style: Clean, well-documented, avoid unnecessary comments unless requested
development_standards.testing_approach: Console commands for quick navigation and testing
development_standards.feature_implementation: Implement fully, then iterate based on feedback

major_milestones: Core physics system with hearse + ragdoll corpse
Complete audio engine with procedural sounds
Head detachment system for escalating chaos
Epic terrain redesign for longer, skill-based journey
project_evolution.current_status: Working demo - technically functional but needs significant refinement in humor, visuals, and challenge
project_evolution.critical_assessment: At crossroads: solid foundation but not compelling enough yet. Risk of abandonment without focused improvement.
project_evolution.github_goal: Create shareable browser-playable game via GitHub Pages
narrative_concepts: Roadkill substitution scene - corpse damaged/missing, driver faces choice to swap in roadkill
Dark ethical choices wrapped in gallows humor
Escalating absurdity balanced against noir solemnity
breakthrough_insights: Comedy = anticipatory 'oh no!' moments when plans visibly fail
Visual identity = consistent details (line thickness, edges, typography, era)
Challenge = failure states with real consequences (pits, bridges, game over)
Skill = mastery through physics stunts, not just careful driving
project_evolution.development_approach: Currently collecting 'cool ideas' to experiment with. Build ONE demo using some ideas, not all. Bigger goal: larger game with many challenges using accumulated concepts.
immediate_priorities: MAJOR DESIGN CONVERSATION - fresh chat for gameplay redesign
Build ONE demo challenge incorporating new failure/comedy concepts
Address critical questions: scope management, failure balance, visual consistency
Experiment with specific challenge types before expanding

atmosphere_concepts: Pixel skull dispatcher - ambiguous underworld entity
Art Deco inspired architecture and visual era
Typography and line thickness defining visual identity
comedy_insights: RV There Yet reference - plans failing in visible, anticipated ways
Cargo falling off cliffs requiring retrieval missions
'Oh no!' moments when you see what's about to happen
critical_questions_pending: Scope management: Too many features vs. one perfect challenge first?
Failure state balance: How punishing? Restart vs. checkpoint?
Visual consistency: Can Art Deco + psychedelic + noir coexist?
Demo focus: Which single challenge type to prototype first?

last_session_achievements: Added teleport commands for testing navigation
active_issues: Hearse physics occasionally locks into 90-degree bounce loops
Buildings sit on uneven terrain instead of flat ground
Head detachment may need threshold tuning (currently rare)
session_memory.collaboration_mood: Highly positive, user appreciates audio system and terrain design
